name: conductor
description: A lightweight orchestration layer that turns Claude Code into a disciplined senior engineering team. Conductor frames any task, plans behind an approval gate, routes each step to the best specialist plugin (or a built-in fallback), and runs a bundled Test Architect plus an adversarial Security Gate before handing back staged, reviewed work explained in plain language. Work lives on a resumable .conductor/ team board (principal → engineer → junior delegation) guarded by a six-criterion A-grade quality gate with Stop-hook teeth, and every task is right-sized to the cheapest model/effort that still holds the bar. Hard, hook-enforced safety rails — proven by a 186-case adversarial battery run in CI on macOS/Linux/Windows across Python 3.9–3.14: never commits, pushes, merges, or publishes; never touches PRODUCTION (blocked even under --dangerously-skip-permissions); opening/merging a PR and preview deploys always pause for explicit human confirmation — enforced on Bash, file writes, and every MCP tool alike (the rails arm via a tiny Python 3 hook; the SessionStart banner is your proof they're live). Stack-agnostic: it detects your project's conventions, conforms, stages the work, and hands off. You ship.
